Question: Which division of the nervous system is concerned with voluntary movement?
Subject: G - Nervous System & Action Potentials
SLO Description: Distinguish between somatic and autonomic nervous system divisions.
Chapter: Chapter 11: Nervous System II – Divisions of the Nervous System
Explanation: The somatic nervous system consists of cranial and spinal motor nerve fibers that transmit voluntary signals from the CNS to skeletal muscles.

Question: Which division of the nervous system is responsible for initiating the fight-or-flight reaction?
Subject: G - Nervous System & Action Potentials
SLO Description: Describe functions of the sympathetic autonomic nervous system.
Chapter: Chapter 11: Nervous System II – Divisions of the Nervous System
Explanation: The sympathetic division prepares the body for stressful emergency or high-energy physical situations ('fight-or-flight').

Question: Which of the following lists the parts of a reflex arc in the correct sequence?
Subject: G - Nervous System & Action Potentials
SLO Description: Trace the pathway of a reflex arc.
Chapter: Chapter 11: Nervous System II – Divisions of the Nervous System
Explanation: A reflex arc proceeds in order: receptor -> sensory neuron -> interneuron (in CNS) -> motor neuron -> effector organ.

Question: Which lobe of the cerebrum is most responsible for higher intellectual functions like problem solving?
Subject: G - Nervous System & Action Potentials
SLO Description: Identify cerebral lobes and their functional specializations.
Chapter: Chapter 11: Nervous System II – Divisions of the Nervous System
Explanation: The frontal lobe houses the prefrontal cortex responsible for planning reasoning decision making and complex cognitive behavior.

Question: What is a major role of the cerebellum?
Option A: Maintaining posture by influencing muscle tone
Option D: Regulating depth and rate of breathing
Correct Answer: Maintaining posture by influencing muscle tone
Subject: G - Nervous System & Action Potentials
SLO Description: Describe functions of the cerebellum in motor control.
Chapter: Chapter 11: Nervous System II – Divisions of the Nervous System
Explanation: The cerebellum coordinates voluntary skeletal muscle movements maintains posture and regulates body balance and muscle tone.

Question: The cell bodies of which type of neuron lie outside the spinal cord in the dorsal root ganglion?
Subject: G - Nervous System & Action Potentials
SLO Description: Identify locations of sensory neuron cell bodies in dorsal root ganglia.
Chapter: Chapter 11: Nervous System II – Divisions of the Nervous System
Explanation: Unipolar sensory (afferent) neuron cell bodies reside outside the spinal cord inside dorsal root ganglia.

Question: Which type of neurons exit the spinal cord through the ventral roots of spinal nerves?
Subject: G - Nervous System & Action Potentials
SLO Description: Describe spinal nerve roots and motor pathways.
Chapter: Chapter 11: Nervous System II – Divisions of the Nervous System
Explanation: Motor (efferent) neuron axons exit the spinal cord via ventral (anterior) roots to innervate peripheral effector organs.

Question: This structure connects the brain and spinal cord and contains nuclei that transmit impulses from the cardiac center to the heart:
Subject: G - Nervous System & Action Potentials
SLO Description: Describe functions of brainstem centers in the medulla oblongata.
Chapter: Chapter 11: Nervous System II – Divisions of the Nervous System
Explanation: The medulla oblongata connects the brain to the spinal cord and houses vital autonomic centers including cardiac vasomotor and respiratory centers.

Question: Which portion of a reflex arc is most likely to be located entirely within the central nervous system?
Subject: G - Nervous System & Action Potentials
SLO Description: Identify components of a reflex arc.
Chapter: Chapter 11: Nervous System II – Divisions of the Nervous System
Explanation: Interneurons (association neurons) reside entirely within the gray matter of the brain and spinal cord (CNS) serving as processing centers in complex reflex arcs.

Question: Which of the meninges is intimately attached to the surface of the brain and spinal cord?
Subject: G - Nervous System & Action Potentials
SLO Description: Describe meningeal layers covering the brain and spinal cord.
Chapter: Chapter 11: Nervous System II – Divisions of the Nervous System
Explanation: The pia mater is the innermost delicate highly vascular meningeal layer that directly adheres to the surface contours of the brain and spinal cord.

Question: The central nervous system is comprised of the brain and ________
Subject: G - Nervous System & Action Potentials
SLO Description: Identify anatomical divisions of the central nervous system.
Chapter: Chapter 11: Nervous System II – Divisions of the Nervous System
Explanation: The central nervous system (CNS) consists of the brain and the spinal cord.

Question: Which brain region located within the diencephalon serves as the primary control center for body temperature hunger thirst and endocrine function?
Subject: G - Nervous System & Action Potentials
SLO Description: Describe functions of diencephalon structures including the hypothalamus.
Chapter: Chapter 11: Nervous System II – Divisions of the Nervous System
Explanation: The hypothalamus is a key homeostatic control center regulating autonomic activities body temperature food/water intake and pituitary gland secretion.

Question: Cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) is produced by specialized networks of capillaries and ependymal cells called
Subject: G - Nervous System & Action Potentials
SLO Description: Describe the formation circulation and function of cerebrospinal fluid.
Chapter: Chapter 11: Nervous System II – Divisions of the Nervous System
Explanation: Choroid plexuses in the brain ventricles secrete cerebrospinal fluid to cushion the brain and transport nutrients.

Question: Which division of the autonomic nervous system promotes 'rest-and-digest' activities such as decreasing heart rate and stimulating digestive tract motility?
Subject: G - Nervous System & Action Potentials
SLO Description: Compare structure and functions of sympathetic and parasympathetic divisions.
Chapter: Chapter 11: Nervous System II – Divisions of the Nervous System
Explanation: The parasympathetic division promotes energy conservation and maintenance activities during restful states ('rest-and-digest').

Question: Which region of the diencephalon acts as the primary sensory relay station sorting and directing incoming sensory impulses to the appropriate cerebral cortex areas?
Subject: G - Nervous System & Action Potentials
SLO Description: Identify functions of the thalamus as a sensory gateway.
Chapter: Chapter 11: Nervous System II – Divisions of the Nervous System
Explanation: The thalamus acts as a gateway to the cerebral cortex relaying all sensory impulses (except smell) to specialized cortical areas.
